competency_groups.title: Electromagnetic Induction Competencies
competency_groups.description: Mastering the principles of electromagnetic induction and its applications.
competencies: Explain Faraday's Law of Electromagnetic Induction
Apply Lenz's Law to determine direction of induced current
Analyze self-inductance and mutual inductance phenomena
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit-IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents

competency_groups.title: Alternating Current Competencies
competency_groups.description: Understanding the fundamentals of alternating currents in circuits.
competencies: Derive expressions for instantaneous and RMS values of AC
Analyze behavior of L, C, and R components in AC circuits
Calculate resonance frequency and Q-factor in series LC circuits
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit-IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents

competency_groups.title: Electromagnetic Waves Competencies
competency_groups.description: Exploring the nature, properties, and spectrum of electromagnetic waves.
competencies: Describe the transverse nature of electromagnetic waves
Explain the relationship between electric and magnetic fields in EM waves
Identify regions of the electromagnetic spectrum based on frequency

competency_groups.title: Ray Optics and Optical Instruments Competencies
competency_groups.description: Applying laws of reflection, refraction, and optical instrument principles.
competencies: Apply Snell's Law to solve problems involving refraction at spherical surfaces
Derive the lens maker's formula for thin lenses
Explain the working principle of microscopes and telescopes

competency_groups.title: Wave Optics Competencies
competency_groups.description: Investigating wave phenomena such as interference, diffraction, and polarization.
competencies: Explain Young's double-slit experiment and derive fringe width formula
Analyze conditions for constructive and destructive interference
Describe the phenomenon of polarization and its applications
